title: Woman charged in Southborough fatal crash
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Abigail Throckmorton, 23, was charged with motor vehicle homicide in a February crash on Turnpike Road in Southborough.
Throckmorton allegedly drove 70 mph on the wrong side, causing a head-on collision that killed Crystal Johnson-Clementi.
Throckmorton pleaded not guilty, was released with conditions, and is set for a pretrial hearing on June 30.

title: What’s causing an engine shake in my 10-year-old SUV?
author: John Paul
bullets: A 2016 Audi Q5 owner reports engine shaking at highway speeds with check engine and EPC lights on.
The Car Doctor suggests engine misfire due to flashing check engine light and advises towing to prevent damage.
EPC light indicates a potential fault in the throttle system or electronics, possibly linked to the engine issue.

title: 5 takeaways as Red Sox fall flat against Rays on Mother’s Day
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Payton Tolle allowed three earned runs and struck out four over five innings in the Red Sox's 4-1 loss to the Rays.
Trevor Story's struggles continued with three strikeouts but managed a double and scored Boston's only run.
Willson Contreras exited with a hand injury, potentially leaving Red Sox thin at first base while he recovers.

title: Boston gang members, associates facing slew of firearm, drug trafficking charges
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Eight members and associates of Boston's Columbia Point Dawgs and Johnston Road gangs indicted for firearm and drug trafficking.
The defendants allegedly sold guns and drugs across Boston and southeastern Massachusetts over a seven-month federal investigation.
Authorities recovered 18 firearms, conversion devices, over 400 grams of fentanyl, and 28 grams of cocaine base.

title: Photos: Scenes from a fun, but soggy, Saturday in Greater Boston
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: LEGO Group's Duckling Day event held on May 9 at Boston Common featured duck-themed activities with LEGO® DUPLO® bricks.
Somerville's PorchFest brought free music to city porches, attracting thousands despite Saturday's rainy weather.
City Hall Plaza hosted the Mojo Boston Music Festival, a 10-hour event celebrating contemporary music and urban art.

title: New Bedford uncle pleads guilty to killing nephew during family altercation
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Juan Padilla-Santana, 43, pleaded guilty to manslaughter for the 2024 stabbing death of his nephew, Omil Padilla-Corsino.
Padilla-Santana was sentenced to 12 ½ to 17 ½ years in prison in Fall River Superior Court.
The fatal altercation occurred in a New Bedford apartment; Padilla-Santana claimed self-defense during the family dispute.

title: 3 takeaways from the Revolution’s 2-1 comeback win against the Philadelphia Union
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The New England Revolution defeated the Philadelphia Union 2-1 after overcoming an early own goal at Gillette Stadium.
Luca Langoni and Carles Gil scored in the second half, pushing New England's home record to 6-0-0 in 2026.
Revolution coach praised defender Will Sands for maintaining his performance despite scoring an own goal.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Matt Damon hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Matt Damon hosted the May 9 episode of 'Saturday Night Live' with musical guest Noah Kahan.
Damon's third hosting of 'SNL' included sketches like a cat litter ad and a 'Mom movie' trailer.
Aziz Ansari returned as FBI Director Kash Patel during a DC bar cold open with Damon as Brett Kavanaugh.

title: Tracy McGrady clarifies comments about Jaylen Brown’s ‘frustration’ with Celtics
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Tracy McGrady stated he had no recent conversations with Jaylen Brown about frustrations with the Celtics.
McGrady clarified comments were based on observations, not direct communication with Brown post-Game 7 loss to 76ers.
Brown expressed continued commitment to Boston, calling his past season a favorite and voicing willingness to stay long-term.

title: Pedro Martinez comments on Red Sox’ decision to reassign Jason Varitek’s role
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Pedro Martinez commented on Jason Varitek's reassignment from his role with the Red Sox, expressing surprise over the decision.
Martinez has not spoken to Varitek since the reassignment but believes his former teammates will find new roles in MLB.
Martinez participated in a ceremony honoring Red Sox legends at Fenway Park, celebrating the team's 125th anniversary of their first home game.
